item.title: The XSS[.]is files: Discussion on the July 2025 events with ex-moderators
item.description:
The XSS files: Discussion on the July 2025 events with ex-moderators

In the past weeks, everyone enough related to the infosec community, have seen big news announcing the “arrest of the administrator of the infamous XSS[.]is forum”, a major blow to a +20 years long history forum and its community.
That’s why I decided to have a brief talk with Anomaly, moderator from the new DamageLib forum and ex-moderator of the XSS forum in representation of the other moderators, to understand and explain these events that happened recently from the insights of the people close related to the forum.

The interview was made in English. Everything said below is the original text. The date of the talk was August 7th, events after this date were not discussed

To make it more understandable and for those who may have missed any information, he setup a timeline of the events, I added images and links:

22.7 — Toha was arrested in Ukraine and his apartment was searched
23.7 — There was first news on REUTERS, then official press-release on Europol website

Reuters news → https://www.reuters.com/technology/suspect-linked-russian-language-cybercrime-forum-arrested-ukraine-2025-07-23/

Europol news → https://www.europol.europa.eu/media-press/newsroom/news/key-figure-behind-major-russian-speaking-cybercrime-forum-targeted-in-ukraine

23.7 — it was late evening — domain xss.is was seized with LE deface, but backup domains — xss[.]as and onion (xssforumv3isucukbxhdhwz67hoa5e2voakcfkuieq4ch257vsburuid[.]onion) continued its work
24.7 — official press-releases from SBU, National Police of Ukraine, Prosecutor’s Office

SBU → https://x.com/g0njxa/status/1948379791503003736
PGO → https://x.com/g0njxa/status/1948386213162795117
NPU → https://x.com/g0njxa/status/1948433517714497947

24.7 — evening xss[.]as stopped its work and started to be redirected to google, onion domain was changed to xssforum7mmh3n56inuf2h73hvhnzobi7h2ytb3gvklrfqm7ut3xdnyd[.]onion with redirect
24.7 — admin gave his first statement and wrote it in stylistics of Toha imitating him

Translated from Russian:

Guys the main server is not affected. All efforts are thrown into replacing the infrastructure. The forum and Jabber may be unavailable for a short time in the near future. Domains being disconnected is a common occurrence. Don’t panic. As soon as I understand the situation, I will return with an answer.

25.7 — second statement — that “Toha is unavailable” but that backend-server is okay and forum will “move” its infrastructure and continues its works, words about “trusted person” who had access to “backend-server” and other infra

Translated from Russian:

Restoring the forum
Toha is unavailable, but this scenario was discussed with him in advance in order to preserve the forum’s functionality despite everything and everyone. Some of the people who helped mantain the forum are not getting in touch. The backend and backups are safe, but part of the infrastructure has been exposed and will require replacement. This won’t affect the forum’s operation. The domain was transferred to the registrar, over time there will be a new one, all old .onion domains will be replaced for security reasons.
New tor domain: xssforum7mmh3n56inuf2h73hvhnzobi7h2ytb3gvklrfqm7ut3xdnyd.onion
WWW domains in progress
The moderators remain in place. Deposit operations are on hold.

Please be patient
Work is currently underway to build new infrastructure.
The initial task was to cut off all possible access points and determine how far they were able to go, what happened in full and now we can say with confidence that they did not reach the main forum server. At the moment, only the new onion is working, in the near future WWW will be raised, but only when the entire infrastructure is completely replaced.
We will resolve issues with deposits, auto guarantors and complaints after everything is set up and transferred.

Forum
- preparation for transfer to a new infrastructure.
Domains
- the clears are ready, I will give them out after the forum transfer
- new tor, new certificate (made just in case), redirect removed (http -> https)
Auto guarantor
- finances are safe
- pending applications will be completed, money will be issued
- After the update, the autoguarantor will work in the standard solution
Deposits
- I analyze the situation

25.7 — moderators started to talk between themselves — as everything looked doubtful — what was the sense to continue operating forum on two domains if admin was arrested and one domain seized? who is doing it? nobody contacted moderators — nobody knew who is this admin. there were first ideas — that LE operation is still going on
25.7 — moderators made a decision — to make whole dump of all forum content except commercial topics — and during next few days they dumped 100% of all content (except commerce)
26.7 — it was decided by all moderators (except three of them with whom nobody had any contact) that we need to create a new forum from a scratch — moderators decided to write custom scripts that will send a PM with a warning to all users that forum is controlled by SBU
26.7 — moderators new forum is opened on onion domain: http://damaga377vyvydeqeuigxvl6g5sbmipoxb5nne6gpj3sisbnslbhvrqd[.]onion/forum
2.8 — from three accounts of moderators started their spam on XSS to warn people in PM that they are on a honey-pot now, 13k+ users successfully got this warning :)

Translated from Russian:

Update according to the situation!
The moderators have an important warning for you: we have not been able to verify the identity of the new admin. In our opinion, Damaga is under the control of French law enforcements and the SBU. He is still online as part of an undercover operation. The goals of this operation are to collect even more evidence on users and make even more arrests! The criminal case concerns not only Tokha, but the entire Damaga, and therefore us.
To avoid further arrests, we have decided to open an alternative and safe platform DamageLib: http://damaga377vyvydeqeuigxvl6g5sbmipoxb5nne6gpj3sisbnslbhvrqd[.]onion/forum/
Clearnet domain: https://damagelib.tw/
We recommend registering on the new forum only under new nicknames! Specify any email (even a non-existent one, there is no verification).
All articles and content from Damaga have already been copied and will be transferred to the forum in the near future.
Thanks for your attention and stay safe!

2.8 — since the same day a powerful DDOS-attack started on a new forum and all moderators were banned (I think except one guy who had just his moderator status removed)
2.8 — XSS closed its forum and put a landing that it’s changing infrastructure
3.8 — opened on onion and new domain (xss[.]pro) — xss[.]pro was hosted (!) on HostLand in St Petersburg, Russia, then they also put a russian flag in XSS logo :) they also registered xss[.]space but for some reason didn’t use xss[.]ms and xss[.]ws that Toha had but never used himself (it was strange too — they had access to backend but not to his domains?)
3.8 — administrator made a statement — where said that jabber server thesecure.biz was compromised, also that he doesn’t have access to deposits on cold wallets that supposedly were seized by SBU, repeated that backend server is safe, didn’t explain who he is but said that moderators are traitors.

Translated from Russian:

Good afternoon.
So, all technical work on restoration and migration to the new infrastructure has been completed.

As soon as I received information about the possible arrest of Tokha, the first priority was to understand how far they had gotten and what data they managed to obtain.

The results of the analysis of the old infrastructure showed the following:

  1. Access to management of the WWW domains had been intercepted; the onion domain might also have been intercepted (this is the reason a new onion domain was created).
  2. The Jabber server was fully compromised. It contained a binary file that allowed retrieval of information about the IP addresses of online users, as well as messages sent to recipients who were offline. Logging of conversations and the history of login IP addresses was disabled on the server, as Tokha had reported to you. However, the server itself was not encrypted.
  3. Backend server access was not obtained, no third-party binaries or logins were found. The server was also not encrypted.
  4. The situation with the wallets is far more depressing. All payments were processed through Bitcoin Core, Litecoin Core, and Monero, which were hosted on separate servers. At the time access was obtained, these held only 1.7 BTC, 156 LTC, and 58 XMR; all large deposits were immediately withdrawn, most likely to cold wallets through complex chains. Unfortunately, it is impossible to fully trace them. Most likely, all coins stored on cold wallets were seized by the SBU.
  5. The SBU could not gain physical access to the Jabber and backend servers, so analysis of network activity was sufficient to determine whether or not a compromise occurred.

As a result of the technical work, the following was done:

  1. All elements of the infrastructure were completely replaced and migrated to other servers (in locations where, for political reasons, special services cannot gain physical access).
  2. New WWW and onion domains were deployed.
  3. All critical infrastructure components were fully encrypted.
  4. The entire network technology stack was rebuilt using modern encryption and routing solutions. This will prevent disclosure of the real IP address of the backend server and nearby nodes. If the IP address of a relay is determined and an attempt is made to compromise it, it will be impossible to access the next server in the chain.
  5. New servers with new wallets were deployed, and the communication between these servers and the main backend server was rebuilt. Coins that were on accessible servers have been transferred.

Now, let’s talk about the fate of the forum itself.
XSS is, first and foremost, a community of technically competent people, not a marketplace. The forum has worked, works now, and will continue to work regardless of surrounding events, actions, and opinions regarding the fate of certain individuals.

As before, the forum will feature paid advertising, paid articles, contests, deposits, and an escrow service. This is primarily necessary to ensure the ability to return deposits to users. I have no need for money myself, but paying out over 55 BTC from my own pocket would be unreasonable, since I am not responsible for their storage.

I have also noticed a large number of accounts in the “scrapper” group that roam the forum and collect all messages and user activity. These accounts will be blocked, and the “scrapper” group will be abolished.

The forum will introduce paid registration for new users without a guarantor. If, during registration, a user specifies a guarantor and the guarantor confirms the invitation, the application will be accepted without payment.

Let’s address the situation with the moderators.
As soon as I isolated the old servers and confirmed that backend access had not been obtained, I contacted the closed moderators’ section, informing them that I was currently busy with analysis and migration. I told them that once everything was ready, I would hold a separate conference with the moderators to discuss how best to handle the deposit issue. They read this and then, on their own initiative, decided to set up their own forum and secretly notify XSS users about it by sending private messages and system notifications with alarming messages about the forum being compromised. That was their choice, and I have drawn my own conclusions about these people. At the moment, all moderators who did this have been banned. I left them an email for feedback if they wish to get in touch.

Because of their actions, I was forced to roll back the database to a backup from 01.08.2025.

I am now opening recruitment for new moderators for each section. If you want to become a moderator, you can send me a private message with your application, stating which section you want to moderate and what relevant experience you have (articles, developments, research). After checking applications and confirming competence in the field, moderator status will be granted.

In the near future, all pending issues with auto-escrow and blacklists will be resolved.

In the current situation, there will be no verification, identification of old nicknames, or similar information. Any such verification is tantamount to suicide.

And I want to remind everyone: do not rely solely on the security of the forum’s infrastructure. Your safety is only in your own hands — do not post your personal emails or contacts on forums or in correspondence, and always adhere to a Zero Trust model.

An example of a moderator banned by the admin account, with a message claiming to contact the address xssemail@2mail.co

4.8 — we made our second statement — XSS again shutted down with landing that they now trying to set-up anti-ddos infra — we are still under heavy ddos

Attention! The XSS forum is controlled by the SBU and has been turned into a honeypot.
The forum administrator was indeed arrested in Kyiv, and the new administrator is an outsider from nowhere.
There is a criminal case not only against Tokha, but the forum itself is under investigation — which means all of us!
The moderator team has launched an alternative and safe platform called DamageLib:
Details can be found in the topic at this link. http://damaga377vyvydeqeuigxvl6g5sbmipoxb5nne6gpj3sisbnslbhvrqd.onion/forum/threads/12/
When registering, use a new nickname. Email is not used — enter anything. If you lose your password, it will be impossible to restore it.

The current look of the former XSS onion domain:

Technical Work

The site is temporarily undergoing technical maintenance.
Please try again later.

Comment:
A DDoS protection system is currently being installed, as certain former forum members are attempting to disrupt the platform’s operation. We will make every effort to restore the forum’s normal functioning.

Let’s move on the discussion:

Who was detained in Ukraine as we saw on the news?

It was Toha (old admin of XSS.IS) or Medvedovskyi Anton (today Krebs published his name) — this information is correct, it’s not a rumor etc
But even if we will read press-release they talked about person who acted as technical administrator, forum arbiter and forum escrow — so the only possible person is Toha — he was the only one with these roles
Moderators never judged any kind of “arbitrages”, not that moderators ever had any access to financial part or technical part as server
more of it — nobody from moderators ever heard that there was some different person — or admin — who is in charge of these operations except Toha himself
He is 37 — that’s information is too correct :)

Anomaly refers to this article:
https://krebsonsecurity.com/2025/08/who-got-arrested-in-the-raid-on-the-xss-crime-forum/
where a discussion is provided about the identity of the individual arrested in Ukraine accused of being the administrator of the XSS forum, finding this individual a 37 years old named Anton Gannadievich Medvedovskiy. Anomaly finds this information true and adds: “Name Medvedovskyi is correct. Avdeev — not, this is a random guy who was framed supposedly by Toha himself.”

Can you summarize the history of Toha on all his years behind forums?

Very shortly: he was in operations of Hack-All.net, lately known as Exploit.in since 2005, since 2016 — he was an operator of TheSecure.biz jabber server — that domain he caught on back-order after it was available to registration (previous owner of jabber-server — Flycracker — was arrested), then for unknown reason Toha left Exploit in 2017 — with statement that he “gave it” to trusted person, to a friend. In September 2018 domain xss.is was registered and Toha re-started Damagelab.org as XSS.is, one of its previous administrators — Ar3s was released in August 2018 and found old backup from 2015.
Last owner of DamageLab.org was Chococream, who disappeared in Spring 2018, if I’m not wrong — in March 2018 — forum stopped its work; previous owner and co-admin Ar3s — Sergey Yarets was arrested in November 2017 in Belarus, in connection to Andromeda case, August 2018 he was release — after he serverd half a year in jail
If we would talk shortly about DamageLab owners it will be like that: Winux (started from winux.net.ru) -> LonelyWolf -> Ar3s -> Chococream -> Toha -> ? (story above)

Why do you think he was detained now after several years?

It’s hard to say — because really this guy is forum administrator for more then 20 years now, and I do not want to speculate :)
But this french investigation seems to be a serious one as it started in 2021 and already in 2024 they moved investigation group Ukraine
Of course we can supposed that it’s really started since some ransomware investigation but as far as I understand (and other guys) — forum is investigated itself as conspiracy and criminal organization
So I supposed that really not only Toha is in this case
But anybody from us as moderators
That was the main concern — why they are “honey-potting” it and keeping online
To collect more data. Something similar happened with Hansa Market in its own time
But I think it was even earlier, for example in a case of carderprofit (but you should check this claim)
When somebody was arrested — and then LE operated for a limited period of time forum as a honey-pot
Supposedly something similar is going again with BF

Information about Hansa Market operation: https://www.wired.com/story/hansa-dutch-police-sting-operation/

Quoting the “already in 2024 they moved investigation group Ukraine” sentence

This date is something I wanted to ask because in April 2024 there was a discussion on the forum about the work on Ukraine, since I know it was allowed until this date after Toha made this statement:

It was never allowed. Work on CIS was always prohibited.

But do you think he was aware of someone bigger that was investigating him or the forum, and decided to start taking more care on this Ukraine issue

The thing is, you know, we can talk a lot about some under-representing of some issues or double-talking, can’t say it well
For example, Ransomware was prohibited from Colonial Pipeline incident
But it never meant that credential accesses market is somehow regulated
When most of the players were ransomware actors
I really doubt that the problem was some crime against Ukrainians :) I think the problem was just overload of Interpol cases with ransomware episodes
that are going directly to XSS
and that is not only my opinion — I think it’s like a common understanding
that really the problem is that nobody prohibited ransomware
It was only you know some — play — that let’s announce that there is no RaaS marketing — then its no RaaS — when really everybody from big RaaS was always presented there — also Toha was an arbiter — of some complaints of RaaS players, including famous complaint on LockBitSupp (with michon)

So the main problem that XSS had was ransomware discussion on the forum and this is a direct cause of the recent events

The problem wasn’t just a discussion but ransomware business
Because we can view that forum was like
Let me find right word
“Infrastructure provider” for ransomware business
A place where people met, made connections and supplied their RaaS ventures with almost fucking everything
I think that was the real problem :)
That is why we decided just to prohibit all the commerce on DamageLib
As it leads to problems or to nowhere

Should we expect more cases against other forums that allow ransomware discussion?

About your question — I think that Exploit forum can be next (just speculation)
Forum should be only a discussion place but it like re-defined itself as marketplace
And you see that a lot of marketplaces they were already fucked in 2021

The people pretending to be the admin of XSS, have not contact any moderator right? Even not a single private message

No, because we are sure it’s LE operation
of SBU Ukraine
People would never do like that — if they want to talk with somebody. They can write PM or suggest to contact somebody in some messenger — but this shit even looked like LE would do

I mean it is pretty obvious, even the day that arrest was announced, PRODAFT say that they were not buying more XSS accounts.

referring to this statement on July 23th: https://x.com/PRODAFT/status/1948009956017422600

I think that’s really a common understanding about whole situation (very rare thing in underground today)
Usually there are still some dreamers or believers
Who can buy some BS
But not that time :)
You can read yourself (and you read it as far as I understood) this long thread on Exploit
It’s obvious how opinion of “last believers” changed to its end to some common understanding
And everybody who argues against it — probably were LE themselves — as all accounts were not noticed in any discussions before
When XSS was online for some short time (between their last statement and ours) — I think XSS online was like 19 users (ridiculously low) — and everybody who supported “new admin” were trolls or CTIs

Talking about the community of people, what made XSS different from other forums?

I think — this approach that content still matters
its name (DamageLab) and history
somebody could also say — Article contests )
and he will be right in some sense — but not about “rewards” than about some you know community effect
when you try to have recognition
by your real knowledge
and not by some trolling or BS
or because somebody is just successful
this thing was really unique
and that what we try to save now with DamageLib

Because the main idea is to reunite everything from the community into this new forum, or no?
I mean, did all the past people accept this change or there will be people that will just let it be and left

the main idea, honestly, is to save people asses from possible arrests
because if they will continue to use old forum — it will be the most probable think that will follow
in last statement, for example, old admin already announced that he wants to make a paid registration on forum )
I doubt that he wants to accept Monero for it )))
smells pretty shitty
and of course — to save our community
it was so commercially centric and it still is
it’s more safe to operate commerce not on forums

I believe that as Toha, more people will be living on Ukraine and possibly being looked by other people. Do you think this people are safe as today?

i think there are two groups of people who has more risks than anybody else:
- people who were registered on XSS and operated any business there but who live in Europe countries, any countries that are in Europol;
- of course — ukrainians themselves.
so may be it sounds funny but mostly we save these groups of people. because SBU wants to fire up them or make “slaves” who will DDOS some political shit for them after :)

Do you saw any not normal activity before the arrest on July 22

surpisingly — not. of course there was some crazy story with this “comedy_club” arbitrage — when some crazy armenian fucker from Moscow promised to launder USDT in amount of 666k but what he really did — used his OKX account and gave to his customer some money that was even dirtier than it was before
as a result — this USDT was locked — and it was very funny arbitrage complaint
anyway, Toha decided that comedy_club is responsible only for 2% that he charged his customer for
some ironic detail — that when this client wrote to Tether — to understand — why USDT was locked — they advised him to contact for details ukrainian CyberPolice
and that was a reason in a first days why people saw some connection
between two of this situations
but I think this cases they are not connected at all
at least it will sound strange to me — if EuroPol has some free 666k to do a stupid shit like that just to find some miserable armenian in Moscow

About DamageLib, who is taking care of being the new admin?

we decided to change all our nicknames — but for any persons from XSS is quite obvious who we are, you can’t change your personality :) let me say this way — technical administrator is the same guy who operated DamageLab file-exchanger — and onion domain is still the same, but all our decisions we make together — just by voting. of course, we do not think that “it’s our forum”, “community belongs to us etc” — no, we just act in the interests of community and many questions we will decide with our own community — in open discussions. and we already try to do that :)

What do you think will happen to Toha?

I think he will cooperate. Just see no reason to fight him this case because either he will be responsible for all of this ransomware money that went through him and nobody even would judge him if he will do that.
And about work with SBU — its his own choice :)
Again — no judgements and speculations :)

Any statement you want to say?

I‘m grilled up, so much stuff to do :) really not — all what I want to say — guys, always be critical and paranoid about everything that’s going on around you, don’t be naive and don’t believe that somebody, who is already charged and is in a deep BS — will think about anybody others ass than his own. don’t be so scared to fucking change everything that you only have — to suppose that everything that you had — was compromised, your favourite forum was compromised, your favourite admin is compromised and now cops have everything and that they are eager to use it against you! opsec is very important, it’s fucking necessary. don’t give any chance to anybody to collect any information on you, don’t use these stupid clearweb criminal jabber servers, this fucking retarded deposits, escrows — this all was created just to collect evidence on you. be smart — use whonix — stay in tor — turn off all fucking js )) and let God save you. Amen

End

Expect more content, if possible
My best wishes for you.

2025 ~ @g0njxa

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with MacSync (ex-mentalpositive)
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

This interview was made in collaboration with friend and amazing MacOS researcher xiu (@osint_barbie). Thank you for all the dedication and passion about the MacOS infosec landscape you share with me.

Let’s see today, a brief talk with MacSync (ex-mentalpositive):
The interview was made in English. Original text is provided below.

MacSync is a just a rebrand project where a new administration took management of what we knew previosuly as “Mac.c Stealer by mentalpositive”. It still has the same MaaS business model and same product. If this is true, why this rebrand happened now?

The old project risked dying from lack of time and funding, so it was purchased and will be developed further without dwelling on past difficulties.

Original announcement of rebranding by previous owner mentalpositive:

Translated:

‼️ Hi, we have done a lot of work and released a new version of our product, but in addition to this, we have had a complete rebranding of the project. Contacts for communication have changed, the topic on exploit has changed too, but most importantly, all the original developers have remained in the project!

What is MacSync Stealer?

MacSync Stealer is a reliable stealer with broad functionality that emphasizes simplicity and effectiveness. Literally, you can start using it immediately after purchase.

Is there a history behind this name MacSync?

There isn’t a special story; the name was chosen because it sounds catchy and memorable, easy to pronounce and stays in the ear

How many people do you think have used MacSync? Approximately

We’re a young project with a small audience — roughly up to three dozen people

Since when has the stealer been operating?

The stealer in its earlier form began operating in April of this year. Since then we’ve accumulated experience and technical solutions that are already implemented or planned for the near future.

What makes MacSync different from other products? In such a crowded market (considering amos/rodrigo), what do you offer your clients to convince them to choose your product? (Features, reliability, support, something else?)

We’re at the beginning of our path, but we believe our strengths are reliability, simplicity, and functionality, plus additional modules and an ambitious team.

How do you view your peers in the macOS space, projects like AMOS or Poseidon (Rodrigo)? Any interactions, fights, or mutual respect you can share?

We haven’t encountered them yet; clients choose what they prefer. Competition is healthy — it pushes everyone to improve and differentiate their products.

One of the reasons to try to make this interview is because I saw your product (Mac.c Stealer by mentalpositive) in use by some newly emerged NFT traffer teams, very satisfied with the functionalities and already with profits. Is your product focused on this market niche (traffer teams) or for other uses?

We’re considering which directions to develop and work in. We welcome proposals and opportunities for growth and cooperation.

Example:

https://medium.com/media/9640360f843e76307a318dc1a75973e9/href
Some your competitors blend stealers with backdoor/botnet capabilities. How do you think about that feature breadth? Do you plan any shifts in your product ahead?

Expansion of functionality is positive; we’re not an exception. Work is underway and a new release will be published soon.

Where do you see macOS threat development heading next, I mean, beyond classic info-stealing? Are backdoors/botnets the next wave, or do you see another one, different vectors for attacks on Macs?

Defensive technologies are improving and integrating AI, making them harder to bypass. But the human factor remains — responsible in roughly 8 out of 10 cases. I expect phishing combined with various vulnerabilities (possibly in specific applications) will be actively used in the coming years.

What changes in Apple’s platform (hardware, OS hardening, ecosystem shifts) have most influenced your plans recently?

Probably the introduction of app notarization policy for macOS 10.14.5 and later. Apple keeps tightening certificate issuance and local security policies.

Did you start on Windows or Linux before focusing on macOS, or was macOS your entry point from day one? What pulled you in that direction?

macOS seemed interesting — that’s the key word. I had experience writing specific code for Linux and Windows. macOS was a blank spot under the hood, so it was time to work in that direction too.

Everyone has a war story. What pitfalls or missteps taught you the most, and what would you tell a younger version of yourself to avoid?

Keep going the same way. Experience and skill grow through trial and error. You must work hard — that’s an unchanging condition for success

How do you see the MacOS market? Is this a good time to work?

There’s no other time but now — we work with current conditions. Apple keeps releasing new products and updates, so the near future won’t be boring.

What should people watch for next from you, anything you want to tease or say something about your plans?

No — we won’t boast about what doesn’t exist yet. You’ll hear about things when there’s something to show.

Does MacSync Stealer works on the CIS countries?

No — that’s a principled position and it will not change.

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track MacSync Stealer?

Let everyone do their job well, and then it will be interesting for everyone.

End

Dear reader,
Remember to check the other interviews at: g0njxa — Medium

Expect more content, if possible.
My best wishes to you ❤

@g0njxa

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with AURA
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

Let’s see today, a brief talk with AURA:
The interview was made in English. Original text is provided below.

What is AURA?

AURA is not just a stealer, it is our team and our customers united together. It is a newly formed but rapidly growing community.

Is there a history behind the name AURA?

During the product development phase, it did not have a name. Our team deliberated on this issue for a long time, and the name was conceived approximately one month before the project launch.
People engage in cybercrime for various reasons; some are interested in wealth and expensive cars, while others seek universal attention. There are many more reasons, but all these people have one thing in common: an aura of success.
That’s how the name AURA was conceived. Simple, concise, and self-explanatory.
Our logo is a crown, the upper ray of which intersects with its base to form the letter A, the first letter of our name.

What makes AURA different from other products?

All stealers have similar functionality because they perform similar tasks. The only difference is in the implementation of certain functions.
In terms of implementation, we clearly stand out in terms of product quality and stability. Our team consists of experienced programmers with many years of development experience. These are not scriptkiddies who stumbled into this business by accident.
Despite their extensive experience, our programmers do not rest on their laurels, but study new technologies every day and continue to make Aura better.

If we don’t focus solely on the technical side, another thing that sets us apart is our prompt and high-quality customer support. We take into account the opinion of each of our customers, and many of our product updates have come about thanks to feedback and requests from our customers. Just recently, one of our customers wrote to us, “I wish everyone would respond as quickly as you do.”

When newcomers come to us, we try to help them understand all the issues. And if we see that a person will not be able to monetize their subscription due to lack of experience, we ask them not to rush into a purchase and to study more materials on the subject. It is important to us that every customer has only a positive experience using the product.

AURA has been accused of being a copy of the infamous Lumma Stealer, even there was some confusion on forums by some research posted there. How different are you from Lumma specifically?

There is a similarity between us, but it is purely visual, and only because both products use tabler (https://tabler.io) as a web template for the interface. Now you understand where this confusion came from?
The myth about code copying was spread by people who made superficial assumptions and did not want to look into the facts in detail. People are interested in scandals and intrigue, so they spread very quickly.
There is a person with the nickname “wizardo” on the xss forum. He reverse engineered our builds in the first days after the launch of our project and, contrary to public opinion, proved that our code base has nothing in common with Lumma, which he had previously researched.
You can read a description of this situation in this tweet — https://x.com/KrakenLabs_Team/status/1952302052928803182, as well as find wizardo’s article on the forum and study it yourself if you are interested.
Apart from wizardo, no one else has done a detailed analysis with real facts; everything else is just highly exaggerated nonsense taken out of thin air.

Is resembling what Lumma did the objective of AURA or does this project have other objectives?

I think my previous answer clearly showed that copying is a myth, and that the external similarity is due solely to the use of the Tabler framework. I can name a couple more stealers who also used and continue to use Tabler, but for some reason did not attract public attention (isn’t that funny?). I think the point is not that someone resembles someone else, but rather what emotions our product evokes in the public.
Usually, attention is focused on promising products, while those that remain in the shadows continue to go unnoticed. I think there will always be a lot of noise, rumors, and gossip around us. Overall, this is even a good thing, as it increases our popularity and more people learn about us.
To sum up this issue, AURA is a completely different project with its own path and goals.

How many people do you think have used AURA? Approximately

The number of customers is confidential information.
I have studied some interviews you have conducted with other projects, as well as analyzed other sources, and can provide some comparison for general understanding.
In less than four months, we have attracted more customers than some other projects have in a year or several years of their existence.
Based on this, I can conclude that we are experiencing explosive growth in popularity.

Since when has AURA been working?

On July 8, 2025, our first trading thread appeared on the xss forum, and later our trading threads appeared on other popular forums such as exploit, hackforums, bhf, and other less popular ones.

Can AURA be the next top product after the fall of other maas products?

Regardless of the successes and failures of other products, our plan is to become the number one project. That’s why we’re here. And we will make every effort to achieve this goal and move forward confidently every day. People who previously used Lumma, StealC, Vidar, and Rhadamantis are coming to us. They try our product and stay with us, which makes us very happy.

Does AURA works on the CIS countries?

Our software does not work in CIS countries. Many people have asked us to remove this restriction and offered us large sums of money, but we have refused every request. AURA does not work and will never work in CIS countries. Our builds include checks for CIS systems (which have already been attempted to be bypassed, but unsuccessfully) as well as IP address checks on our servers for additional protection.

How do you see the market? Is this a good time to work?

The market is constantly changing, with local ups and downs in activity, but the overall trend is that this business is thriving and people are working. As for the second part of the question, we believe that the best time to act is here and now, regardless of the surrounding circumstances.

Is there a big effort on the development side?. What will be the future of AURA?

I am a realist and am not used to guessing what will happen next; we have clear plans that we stick to. A huge amount of effort and resources are being put into development, and this should yield results. I myself am interested to see how far AURA can go.

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track AURA?

I would like to extend a warm welcome to the security experts❤️
We have always enjoyed reading their technical reports on various types of malware, and our team will be particularly interested in reading reports about our product in the future.
Although we are effectively on opposite sides of the battlefield, I have a very positive attitude toward them, and I have several friends who are cybersecurity experts.

At the moment, we have a code virtualization module ready, which we have not yet specifically implemented in our builds so that they can be researched. After several interesting technical reports appear, we will completely virtualize our code, and analyzing it will become a very difficult task.

There are many updates ahead and a lot of material for security experts.

End

Dear reader,
Remember to check the other interviews at: g0njxa — Medium

Expect more content, if possible.
My best wishes to you ❤

@g0njxa — 2025

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with XFILES (DeerStealer)
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

Let’s see today, a brief talk with XFILES (aka DeerStealer, a name given by researchers and later implemented in the marketing of XFILES):

The interview was made in Russian. Original answers and English translations are provided below.

How would you describe XFILES?

Коробка с инструментами для быстрого заработка
A box with tools for quick earnings

Is there a history behind the name XFILES?

Нам просто нравится тематика связанная с секретами :)
We just like the subject related to secrets :)

What makes XFILES different from other products?

У нас огромное количество инструментов: Stealer, Spyware, LNK Builder, PowerShell buillder, ClickFix scripts, Unique Crypt по низким ценам, и многое другое!
We have a huge number of tools: Stealer, Spyware, LNK Builder, PowerShell builder, ClickFix scripts, Unique Crypt at low prices, and much more!

I see XFILES is not only a product for Windows, but also offered stealer capabilities in MacOS. How this work?

В данный момент мы работаем над Spyware под macOS! Не расскажем как реализовано чтобы избежать обнаружения! Скажем только то, что он превосходит конкурентов на рынке macOS на десяток голов выше!
We are currently working on Spyware for macOS! We will not tell you how it is implemented to avoid detection! Let’s just say that it surpasses competitors in the macOS market by a dozen heads higher!

Recently it was announced the implementation of AI (Artificial Intelligence) to generate personal proxies. How this work? Is AI helpful in the infostealers development?

Всё что мы можем сказать — мы используют локальную uncensored модель! Помогает в управлении инфраструктурой, главное не давать AI слишком много обязанностей
All we can say is that we are using a local uncensored model! It helps in managing the infrastructure, the main thing is not to give AI too many responsibilities.

How many people do you think have used XFILES? Approximately

Не скажем. Как видите мы имеем рекламу на многих форумах, значит откуда то деньги на это берутся, значит какое то количество клиентов мы имеем :)
We won’t tell. As you can see, we have ads on many forums, which means that the money for this comes from somewhere, which means that we have a certain number of clients :)

Since when has XFILES been working?

2021

Is the development of XFILES focused on the Stealer capabilities, or on other directions such as resident loader, HVNC, etc… ?

Сейчас активно занимаемся разработкой Spyware
We are currently actively developing Spyware.

Does XFILES works on the CIS countries?

Нет и не будет.
No, and it won’t be.

How do you see the market? Is this a good time to work?

1. Раньше было лучше. Это выражение может использоватся каждый год!
2. Но на самом деле сейчас самое лучшее время, так-как криптовалюты выросли в цене, охота на них намного выгоднее чем раньше!
1. It used to be better. This expression can be used every year!
2. But in fact, now is the best time, as cryptocurrencies have increased in value, hunting for them is much more profitable than before!

On this year some of the top infostealer products have disappeared because of different reasons. Do you think the MaaS model is at risk?

Нет, но участники находящиеся за пределамии СНГ — под угрозой!
No, but the participants located outside the CIS are under threat!

What will be the future of XFILES?

«Светлым на тёмной стороне»
“Light on the dark side”

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track XFILES?

Touch grass… at least sometimes!

End

Dear reader,
Remember to check the other interviews at: g0njxa — Medium

Expect more content, if possible.
My best wishes to you ❤

@g0njxa — 2025

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with Phexia
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

This interview was made in collaboration with friend and amazing MacOS researcher xiu (@osint_barbie). Thank you for all the dedication and passion about the MacOS infosec landscape you share with me.

Let’s see today, a brief talk with Phexia:
The interview was made in Russian. Original text and English translations are provided below.

Editor notes: Thread about the community findings on this MacOS Stealer to add context to this interview

https://medium.com/media/26b5502f4f9b258f56034f148d076c79/href

Is there a name for your MacOS Stealer? What is the meaning of it?

В самом старте не было конкретного названия, ибо оно и не требовалось. Проект был полностью закрытым и писался для нескольких человек под их задачи. Потом я придумал название, чтобы люди могли хоть как нибудь разделять мой и другие проекты
At the very start, there was no specific name because it wasn’t needed. The project was completely closed and written for a few people for their tasks. Later, I came up with a name so that people could somehow distinguish my project from others.

The name “Phexia” — what’s the story behind it?

Название «Phexia» было придумано случайно, почти сразу как я задумался о нем. Никакой истории, абсолютно чистый лист для нового проекта
The name “Phexia” was invented randomly, almost immediately when I thought of it. No story, a completely clean slate for a new project.

How many people do you think have used this stealer? Approximately

На данный момент моим продуктом пользуются в районе 20 человек, и я думаю, что это не предел
Currently, about 20 people use my product, and I think this is not the limit.

Since when has the stealer been operating?

Мой проект функционирует с апреля 2024 года, он был приватным решением для пользователей mac.c, которым не хватало функционала MaaS версии
My project has been operating since April 2024; it was a private solution for mac.c users who lacked the functionality of the MaaS version.

Analysis done to your product shows ~85% code similarity with Mac.c stealer. Can you clarify the relationship between your project and mac.c by mentalpositive?

Да, код действительно схож, но он схож только с модулем стиллера, больше абсолютно никакой схожести, кроме палитры цветов панели. Мы были вместе с audi (первоначальный владелец mac.c) и mentalpositive (это кодер обновленного mac.c) знакомыми. Мы занимались разными проектами, и для macOS в том числе. Но мне никогда не нравились их подходы в mac.c, а я просто не мог ничего поменять в коде, поэтому открыл приватное решение, согласовав это с ними. Если быть честным, mac.c был просто ужасным, я видел все дыры и недоработки, которые мог исправить за пару часов, но меня просто не допускали к исправлениям. Но если быть честным, даже это ужасное исполнение, как мне кажется, удовлетворяло клиентов. Как я открыл свой приватный проект я наконец-то смог сделать все лучше.
Yes, the code is indeed similar, but it is only similar to the stealer module, there is absolutely no other similarity except the color palette of the panel. We were acquaintances with audi (the original owner of mac.c) and mentalpositive (the coder of the updated mac.c). We worked on different projects, including for macOS. But I never liked their approaches in mac.c, and I simply couldn’t change anything in the code, so I opened a private solution, coordinating this with them. To be honest, mac.c was just terrible; I saw all the holes and shortcomings that I could fix in a couple of hours, but I was simply not allowed to make corrections. But to be honest, even this terrible implementation, as I think, satisfied clients. When I opened my private project, I was finally able to make everything better.

What makes your stealer different from other products?

Мне кажется, посмотреть на разницу стоит клиентам. Я не смогу объективно оценить её, но исходя из слов клиентов, которые пользовались или пользуются другими решениями на рынке для macOS: мой проект удобнее и работает стабильнее.
I think clients should look at the difference. I can’t objectively assess it, but based on the words of clients who have used or are using other solutions on the market for macOS: my project is more convenient and works more stably.

Why you choose to work on the private instead of a MaaS model?

Потому что хотелось представить людям альтернативу без надобности оплаты на старте. Я выдал несколько подписок абсолютно бесплатно людям, чтобы они не теряли macOS трафик. Мой проект не совсем про заработок, но врать не буду, что собираюсь переходить на MaaS модель, но сделать количество слотов ограниченными. Лучше я буду зарабатывать меньше, но смогу предоставить каждому клиенту самую лучшую поддержку на всех этапах работы.
Because I wanted to offer people an alternative without the need to pay upfront. I gave several subscriptions completely free to people so they wouldn’t lose macOS traffic. My project is not really about making money, but I won’t lie that I plan to switch to a MaaS model but with a limited number of slots. I’d rather earn less but provide each client with the best support at all stages of work.

You implemented DDR (Dead Drop Resolver) via Telegram and Steam profiles — why this method over traditional C2?

Я использую DDR по причине того, что постоянно меняю домены, чтобы продукт лучше работал и не был ограничен антивирусами. Так удобнее, мне кажется. Но еще помимо Telegram и Steam у нас есть получение C2 напрямую с Blockchain. Если бы я указывал в билде C2, то после возможной блокировки сервера всем пришлось бы менять бинарные файлы, а это очень нервный процесс. Поэтому было принято решение реализовать метод, при котором людям не нужно ничего делать при блокировке доменов. Боты просто переподключаются к новым доменам.
I use DDR because I constantly change domains so that the product works better and is not limited by antiviruses. It seems more convenient to me. But besides Telegram and Steam, we also get C2 directly from the Blockchain. If I specified C2 in the build, then after a possible server block, everyone would have to change binary files, which is a very stressful process. Therefore, it was decided to implement a method where people don’t need to do anything when domains are blocked. Bots just reconnect to new domains

Your project is based on a resident MacOS Stealer but also a Reverse Shell functionality with botnet and backdoor capabilities. We can observe the same on other products, do you think this is a trend now beyond classic info-stealing or there will be new directions to target MacOS?

У нас резидентный бот, который имеет в себе модули стиллера и reverse-shell. Я выбрал модульную систему по причине того, что в будущем я смогу создавать новые интересные модули, которые будут полезны моим клиентам. Я считаю, что за дополнительными возможностями будущее, поэтому расширяю функционал своего продукта во всех направлениях.
We have a resident bot that includes stealer and reverse-shell modules. I chose a modular system because in the future I will be able to create new interesting modules that will be useful to my clients. I believe the future lies in additional capabilities, so I am expanding the functionality of my product in all directions.

What’s your background — previous malware projects, legitimate software development, or self-taught? Have you worked with Windows/Linux before?

Я много лет занимался разработкой всякого ПО, но конкретно malware софт начал разрабатывать в 2021 году. У меня никогда не было MaaS проектов, я всегда держал свой софт в привате и поддерживал его для пары человек. В какой-то момент, когда mentalpositive показал мне свои наработки для macOS — я понял, что я влюбился в эту систему и хочу работать только с ней, мне нравится познавать что-то новое.
I have been developing various software for many years, but I started developing malware specifically in 2021. I never had MaaS projects; I always kept my software private and supported it for a few people. At some point, when mentalpositive showed me his developments for macOS, I realized I fell in love with this system and want to work only with it; I like learning something new.

It has been observed how your C2 domains burn incredibly fast , how are you dealing with takedowns? Does it disrupt your operations in the long time?

Домены блокируют часто, но на меня или клиентов это никак не влияет вообще. Боты просто переподключаются к новым прокладочным серверам. Мне лично это никак не мешает, у меня автоматическая система, которая контролирует их блокировку и уведомляет об этом меня, а я их быстро меняю на работоспособные.
Domains are often blocked, but it doesn’t affect me or clients at all. Bots just reconnect to new proxy servers. Personally, it doesn’t bother me; I have an automatic system that monitors their blocking and notifies me, and I quickly change them to working ones.

Are AV-solutions or EDR a problem on the victims that run your stealer? How do you deal with them to get a higher conversion of clicks to log?

Сейчас мы готовим новое обновление, которое будет включать в себя средства обхода антивирусов. Хоть и не так много людей используют антивирусы для macOS, искать их обход все равно стоит. Поэтому, мне буквально похуй на них, в новом обновлении я просто избавлюсь от проблем с ними.
We are currently preparing a new update that will include antivirus bypass tools. Although not many people use antiviruses for macOS, it’s still worth looking for ways to bypass them. So, frankly, I don’t care about them; in the new update, I will simply get rid of problems with them.

How do you see the MacOS market? Is this a good time to work?

macOS очень интересная. Мне нравится с ней работать и думаю, что это направление перспективное благодаря ложным заявлениям «macOS самая безопасная система и на ней нет вирусов»
macOS is very interesting. I enjoy working with it and think this direction is promising due to the false claims that “macOS is the safest system and has no viruses.”

Can you share what improvements we are going to see in Phexia?

Уже в ближайшие дни выйдет крупное обновление, которое сделает мой софт неубиваемым. А еще, поделюсь секретом, мы сделаем фейки Ledger и Trezor доступными для всех без необходимости их отдельной покупки.
A major update will be released in the coming days that will make my software unkillable. Also, I’ll share a secret: we will make fake Ledger and Trezor available to everyone without the need for separate purchase.

Does your stealer works on the CIS countries?

Нет, Phexia не работает в странах СНГ и никогда не будет.
No, Phexia does not work in CIS countries and never will.

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track your stealer?

Я ничего плохого по отношению к экспертам по безопасности не имею, они двигают рынок вперед. Если бы не они, то рынок не развивался бы так стремительно. Они пытаются бороться с нами, но сами того не понимая дают нам сильный толчок в развитии. Если они хотят помочь людям, то им следует держать свои исследования в секрете, а не выставлять их в публичном поле.
I have nothing bad to say about security experts; they push the market forward. Without them, the market wouldn’t develop so rapidly. They try to fight us but unknowingly give us a strong push in development. If they want to help people, they should keep their research secret, not expose it publicly.

End

Dear reader,
Remember to check the other interviews at: g0njxa — Medium

Expect more content, if possible.
My best wishes to you ❤

@g0njxa

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with MioLab (NovaStealer)
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

This interview was made in collaboration with friend and amazing MacOS researcher xiu (@osint_barbie). Thank you for all the dedication and passion about the MacOS infosec landscape you share with me.

Let’s see today, a brief talk with MioLab:
The interview was made in English. Original text is provided below.

What is MioLab?

mac os stealer

Is there a history behind this name MioLab?

just a random letters

How many people do you think have used MioLab? Approximately

victims? about 16,000

Since when has the stealer been operating?

november 2025

Researchers have put a name to your software, Nova Stealer. Are you aware of this name?

no

When I firstly told him about it, he asked: ‘how they give us this name? What were they guided by?’. I replied, completely ignorant, “because its a new stealer so Nova (new) Stealer”. The message was followed by a “xd” and a “haha” of MioLab

What makes MioLab different from competitors? What do you offer your clients to convince them to choose your product?

24/7 support, a lot of huge updates 99% uptime and 99% knocking

In relation to MacOS traffic, why the Clickfix scripts have become so popular in the recent time?

Cloudflare popularized this method. They wanted to make things better for users, but instead, they made things better for us.

In the past months we can observe a popularization of the distribution of MacOS stealers builds via curl payloads, in a “Clickfix” style. MioLab insisted on the promotion of this delivery method while promoting this service, creating utilities inside the panel to generate curl payloads

On one of your promo video there’s an IDA Pro icon visible.
Do you actively reverse-engineer other macOS malware or Apple components, or was this used mainly to test anti-analysis and detection evasion in MioLab?

yes i am reverse everything what i see

What’s your background — previous malware projects, legitimate software development, or self-taught? Have you worked with Windows/Linux before?

I worked at an IT giant, combining log checking

What changes in Apple’s platform (hardware, OS hardening, ecosystem shifts) have most influenced your work recently?

No system is secure and no security updates will protect you from stupidity

Can you share what improvements we are going to see in MioLab in the future?

RAT on Mac OS

Do you offer custom features or modifications to MioLab on request, or is the functionality the same for all customers? If yes, are there features you refuse to implement, even on special request?

functionality the same for all customers, special individual requests only for money

do you notice any antivirus/edr software installed on victims’ systems?
If yes, are there specific products you see more often than others?

no

Do you run and maintain the backend yourself, or is it partially outsourced or automated?

by myself

Do you work on Miolab alone, or is there a small team behind it?

i am alone but i have sup

Do you use AI tools or AI agents in development? Do you think AI will change how macOS malware is built and operated in the next year or is it still more hype than value right now?

no, code written by my hands

Does MioLab works on the CIS countries?

No, and never will be, not for any amount of money. It’s a matter of honor.

How do you see the MacOS market? Is this a good time to work?

Don’t expect good times.

Where do you see macOS threat development heading next, I mean, beyond classic info-stealing. Are backdoors/botnets the next wave, or do you see another one, different vectors for attacks on Macs?

Mac OS botnets will be expensive, we are currently working on this

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track MioLab?

Thank you for your free bug search, you make our product better

End

Dear reader,
Remember to check the other interviews at: g0njxa — Medium

Expect more content, if possible.
My best wishes to you ❤

@g0njxa

item.title: Approaching stealers devs: a brief interview with notnullOSX
item.description:
To completely understand what’s going on in a market that has been growing in the last years I found mandatory to know which players are dominating it. Always remember that behind every user of the Internet there is another human like you, so if you can be kind enough to reach them and they agree, you can have a little talk. Asking things is not a crime.

Please note everything that stated on this blog has only an informational purpose. I will never promote the use of these products.

This interview was made in collaboration with friend and amazing MacOS researcher xiu (@osint_barbie). Thank you for all the dedication and passion about the MacOS infosec landscape you share with me.

Please find some context about this malware here: https://moonlock.com/notorious-hacker-returns-notnullosx-stealer

Let’s see today, a brief talk with notnullOsX:
The interview was made both in English and Russian. Original text is provided below.

What is notnullOSx?

NotnullOSx is less a product and more a concept. It shows that even systems considered safe are only as strong as the assumptions behind them.

notnullOSx — это скорее не продукт, а концепция. Он показывает, что даже системы, которые считают безопасными, защищены ровно настолько, насколько верны лежащие в их основе предположения.

Is there a history behind this name notnullOSx?

Yes, there is actually a lot of meaning and history behind this name, but I would rather leave it for the community. Maybe they will figure it out themselves. One person already solved this “riddle”.

Да, причем в этом нике смысла и истории очень много, но я бы хотел оставить это для комьюнити, возможно они сами поймут смысл этого ника. Один человек уже разгадал эту “загадку”))

How many people do you think have used notnullOSx? Approximately

Fewer than you think. More than you would be comfortable with. At the moment, around 25 active clients with a monthly subscription.

Меньше, чем вы думаете. И больше, чем вам хотелось бы. На данный момент около 25 активных клиентов которые приобрели месячную подписку

Since when has the stealer been operating?

Since March 3, 2026.

3 марта 2026 года

What makes notnullOSx different from competitors? What do you offer your clients to convince them to choose your product?

We are creators, not opportunists. We are not chasing quick money. We are building an ecosystem — a seamless architecture that already proves itself through easy module integration, instant panel deployment, and fast hotfix updates.

We are also the only ones with a working Safari password module under the hood. We are still deciding how to deliver it to clients, because including something like that in a $4k subscription would be unreasonable.

Мы команда созидателей, нам не нужны деньги здесь и сейчас. Мы строим экосистему, бесшовную архитектуру которая уже принесла свои плоды в виде легкости добавления новых модулей, молниеносной установке панелей, таким же быстрым обновлением панелей с hot фиксами критических проблем. У нас у единственных есть под капотом модуль, который крадет Safari пароли, пока у нас идут дискусии в каком виде его подавать клиентам, ибо включать такой модуль в подписку за 4k$ — глупость.

Why did you decide to release a stealer in Go language?

I just like it. But we might move to another language soon to reduce build size.

Он мне нравится, но возможно в скором времени мы перейдем на другой язык, чтобы уменьшить вес билда.

Do you leverage AI for developing? How much of the original codebase was AI-assisted?

AI is a perfect tool in the right hands. It helps with ideas and speeds up development when you hit a dead end.

We never trusted it to write actual stealer modules — everything core is written by us. There was one attempt to generate a simple Firefox password decryptor with Claude, but it refused, so we didn’t push it further. We know those limitations can be bypassed, but honestly we just coded it ourselves in about 30 minutes.

Some parts of the web panel, maybe 15 to 20 percent, were AI-assisted.

AI — это идеальный инструмент в хороших руках. Он ускоряет разработку креативов, помогает развить мысли когда ты зашел в тупик. Писать код для модулей стиллера мы ему еще никогда не доверяли, все пишем сами. Насколько я знаю, была какая то попытка написать простенький декрипт Firefox паролей с помощью Claude, но он отказал в выполнении, и мы не стали дальше его мучать, хотя на 100% знаем, что эти ограничения легко обойти, в итоге сами за полчаса это накодили. Но некоторые элементы веб панели (15–20%) — писал AI

Are you working alone, or do you have coders involved this time?

No one really works alone. Some contributors are just less visible.

Никто по-настоящему не работает один. Просто некоторые участники менее заметны.

What advantages do you see in the modular architecture, downloading and staging individual binaries per function rather than shipping everything in one binary?

Flexibility always outlives monoliths.

Гибкость всегда переживает монолиты.

We saw the Firebase endpoint mactest-6b2ab-default-rtdb[.]firebaseio.com used in the code. Why did you choose Firebase?

Sometimes the most obvious places are the least suspicious. Firebase is just one of several communication methods we rotate for better stealth.

Иногда самые очевидные места оказываются самыми незаметными. Firebase это лишь один из некоторых способов клиент-серверного соединения, наш продукт чередует еще несколько способов, для более незаметной работы

In the code we observed the path /Users/angola/Documents/mactest/modules/telegram_grab.go. What is Angola, is it nickname of your ’teammate-’coder, a project codename or something else?

It’s nothing special. Just the admin account name on one of our macOS VPS servers that was located in Africa. First thing that came to mind was “angola”.

Ха-ха, это просто название admin аккаунта одной из наших macOS VPS, которая находилась в Африке, и первое что пришло в голову при выборе названия аккаунта, это почему то — angola 😆

Is notnullOSx designed to function as a RAT beyond data collection, or is the channel currently used only for exfiltration and callbacks?

Definitions depend on perspective. We already have a solid reverse shell, but we want to expand functionality before fully calling it a RAT.

Определения зависят от точки зрения. У нас уже есть хороший reverse-shell, но мы хотим добавить больше функциональности чтобы иметь 100% право называть продукт — RAT

Do you plan to add iCloud credential exfiltration modules?

This is one of the main directions we are currently focusing on.

Это одно из главных направлений куда мы сейчас развиваемся

Can you share what improvements we are going to see in notnullOSx in the future?

Smaller footprint. Smarter behavior. Less visibility. And Safari passwords, of course.

Меньше следов. Умнее поведение. Меньше заметности. И Safari пароли конечно

Does notnullOSx works on the CIS countries?

Absolutely not.

Ни в коем случае

How do you see the MacOS market? Is this a good time to work?

It is underestimated, and that makes it interesting. Competition is almost nonexistent, mostly script kiddies.

If you compare feedback, it becomes obvious. I once had a short conversation with a competitor and realized immediately how easy this space is. The guy didn’t even understand what he was talking about and was running his panel behind Cloudflare, basically sending his clients’ data straight to law enforcement.

Он недооценен. Именно это делает его интересным. Конкуренция на нуле. Только скрипт кидди. Если сравнить мои отзывы на форуме, с отзывами других участников рынка — все становится понятно. У меня был один диалог с моим “конкурентом”, он отправил несколько сообщений, и я понял, что игра слишком легкая 😆, человек вообще не понимает о чем говорит, и держит панель на Cloudflare (LOL), чтобы данные его клиентов летели прямо в руки ФБР и Интерполу

Where do you see macOS threat development heading next, I mean, beyond classic info-stealing. Are backdoors/botnets the next wave, or do you see another one, different vectors for attacks on Macs?

Botnets on macOS are not very practical. I think everything is moving toward more sophisticated ways of stealing cryptocurrency.

От macOS ботнета смысла мало, я думаю все идет к более изощренным способам воровства криптовалюты.

What would you say to those “information security experts” who are trying to track notnullOSx?

If you are looking for something obvious, you are already too late.

You can write a million articles, try to trace C2 infrastructure, it doesn’t really cause any damage. I actually have good relationships with some researchers. There is mutual respect, and sometimes I even help them understand parts of the system.

It’s a cat and mouse game. If they find something today, it will be gone in the next build.

Если вы ищете что-то очевидное, вы уже опоздали. Вы можете писать миллион статей о том, как работает notnullOSx, пытаться палить IP наших c2 серверов, просто это не наносит никакого урона. Я со многими ресерчерами в хороших отношениях, у нас есть взаимное уважение, и я даже иногда помогаю в ресерче моего продукта. Это ведь кошки-мышки, ресерчер найдет за что можно зацепиться — в следующем билде уже этой зацепки не будет. И так каждый день.

End
